title: Ironsworn: Starforged - part 2 - session recap and detailed look at the oracles (solo RPG)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QhRPaWYjWYc

Daniel (@dungeondive) returns with the middle entry of the Starforged trilogy: a recap of his first sessions as Brother Quint, followed by a working tour of the game's Oracle system. The narrative half tracks Quint's plague investigation outward from Rock Home Port — a Set a Course move turned up the keywords "abandoned protection," which led to Astrid Ruiz, an aging Union merchant at Portent Station who knows of an unmapped trade route to Damu but is being hunted by a debt collector named Saktaro. A Compel move secures her cooperation; the pair sneak aboard the Jackdaw and depart on the expedition.

The second half is a chart-by-chart tour of the reference guide. Action + Theme for general drama prompts, Descriptor + Focus for locations, deeper tables for stellar objects, planets (each with its own atmosphere/feature/peril subtables), spaceborne perils, settlements, starships, characters, creatures keyed to a difficulty ladder, factions with quirks and rumours, derelicts that function as space dungeons, and precursor vaults with eight location themes. Daniel describes the architecture as a nesting doll of mechanism and fiction — compelling once you find the right layer, but occasionally paralysing while you're hunting for it.

----

Daniel calls Starforged a "nesting doll" of mechanism and fiction — when you're stuck mid-session, do you find deep oracle systems like Starforged's a creative lifeline, or do they slow you down compared to a single-page oracle?
